mnemonics: FUSE for Requirement Modeling Strategies: Flow (scenarios), Understanding (classes), States (behavior), Elicitation (structured)
keyPoints: Flow-Oriented Modeling: Uses data flow diagrams (DFDs) to represent how data moves through a system.
Class-Based Modeling: Identifies objects, their attributes, and their relationships using class diagrams.
Behavioral Modeling: Represents the system's state-dependent behavior using state diagrams and sequence diagrams.
commonMistakes: Focusing on only one modeling strategy instead of using a combination for a complete view.
Creating overly complex models that are difficult for stakeholders to understand and validate.
Confusing data flow diagrams (process-oriented) with flowcharts (control flow-oriented).
